pattern: Once a set of values has been chosen to negate, the lexicographically smallest arrangement of that fixed multiset is always just sorting it -- so the only real decision is which values to make negative, and the biggest value should be negated first whenever that still leaves the rest able to reach the target.
hints: Whatever values end up negative, arranging any fixed multiset of numbers into its lexicographically smallest sequence is simply sorting it in increasing order -- so the whole problem reduces to deciding which values get a minus sign.

The most negative number possible is always -n, and making a large value negative can only make the eventual sorted sequence's front smaller, never larger -- so greedily try to negate the largest remaining value first.

Only skip negating the current largest value if doing so would make it impossible for the smaller remaining values to make up the rest of the required sum; check this with a simple running total and the maximum sum the smaller values could still contribute.

edge_inputs.description: smallest n, target reachable only by negating everything
target equal to the sum of all positive values, nothing negated
target at the most negative possible value, everything negated
target one unit outside the achievable range
target has the wrong parity relative to the total, so no arrangement can ever work
a target achievable only by negating some, but not the largest, values
target of zero requires an exact balanced split

stress.description: random n with a target chosen from the achievable range so a real answer usually exists
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    total = n * (n + 1) // 2
    t = rng.randint(0, total)
    target = total - 2 * t
    return [n, target]
